name: Detour to Spúlveda — circular through the Natural Park Hoces del río Duratón
name_original: Camino de rodea a Spúlveda — circular por el Parque natural Hoces del río Duratón
region_intro: Jogging around Sepúlveda offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Duratón River and its natural park. The region features river gorges, limestone cliffs, and open countryside, providing varied terrain for runners. These routes allow for exploration of the area's natural beauty and historical elements. The trails range from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Sepúlveda The most popular jogging route is Long Duratón Trail, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 35 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the scenic Duratón River. Another top favourite among local runners is Detour to Spúlveda — circular through the Natural Park Hoces del río Duratón, a Moderate 4.5 miles (7.2 km) path. This route winds through the natural park, offering views of river gorges and varied landscapes. Local runners also love the Long Trail of the Duratón River – Vultures' Nest loop from Parque Natural de la Hoces del Río Duratón, a 11.7 miles (18.9 km) trail leading through the Natural Park Hoces del río Duratón, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Jogging around Sepúlveda is defined by river valleys, natural park landscapes, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more difficult running trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Sepúlveda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sepúlveda?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Sepúlveda listed on komoot, catering to various fitness levels. These include easy riverside paths, moderate trails through the natural park, and more challenging long-distance runs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Sepúlveda?
A: Yes, Sepúlveda offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164015682/long-duraton-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Duratón Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.9 miles (4.6 km) long and follows the scenic Duratón River.

Q: What are the typical distances of running trails around Sepúlveda?
A: The running trails around Sepúlveda vary significantly in distance. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164015682/long-duraton-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Duratón Trail&lt;/a&gt; at 2.9 miles (4.6 km), moderate options such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164034373/detour-to-spulveda-circular-through-the-natural-park-hoces-del-rio-duraton" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Detour to Spúlveda — circular through the Natural Park Hoces del río Duratón&lt;/a&gt; at 4.5 miles (7.2 km), and longer, more challenging runs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Trail of the Duratón River – Vultures' Nest loop from Parque Natural de la Hoces del Río Duratón&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 11.7 miles (18.9 km).

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sepúlveda?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sepúlveda are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164034373/detour-to-spulveda-circular-through-the-natural-park-hoces-del-rio-duraton" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Detour to Spúlveda — circular through the Natural Park Hoces del río Duratón&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied landscapes within the natural park.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I expect along the running trails?
A: The running trails in Sepúlveda often provide stunning views, especially within the Hoces del Duratón Natural Park. You can expect to see dramatic river gorges and limestone cliffs. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6485442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Virgen de la Peña&lt;/a&gt; and various spots offering &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5791277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of vulture nests&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Sepúlveda?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in the Sepúlveda area, especially within the Hoces del Duratón Natural Park, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always check specific park regulations or route details for any restrictions.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while running in Sepúlveda?
A: Running in Sepúlveda offers access to several natural attractions. The region is defined by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5229100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Duratón Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, where you can witness impressive gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Tabladillo&lt;/a&gt; and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3706411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Cliff&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter unique geological formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5229115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Seven Altars&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Sepúlveda?
A: The best time for running in Sepúlveda is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially midday, while winters can be cold, though trails generally remain accessible.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in Sepúlveda?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Sepúlveda, particularly near popular trailheads and access points to the Hoces del Duratón Natural Park. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: Are there any wheelchair-accessible running paths in Sepúlveda?
A: While many trails in the Sepúlveda area feature varied terrain and some ascents, some sections along the Duratón River might offer flatter, more accessible surfaces. However, dedicated wheelchair-accessible running paths are not widely available. It's best to check individual route descriptions for specific accessibility information.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sepúlveda?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse and stunning natural scenery of Sepúlveda, particularly the dramatic river gorges and the abundance of wildlife, including vultures. The variety of routes, from gentle riverside paths to challenging climbs, also receives positive feedback, making it suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any hidden gem running trails in Sepúlveda?
A: While the main trails in the Hoces del Duratón Natural Park are well-known, exploring less-trafficked paths can reveal hidden gems. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Andrés – Plaza de España, Cantalejo loop from Cantalejo&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 4.9-mile (7.9 km) run through varied landscapes slightly outside the immediate Sepúlveda center, providing a different perspective of the region.
tips: One of the most beautiful towns in this area of the Iberian Peninsula and located in the middle of the Natural Park of Las Hoces del Río Duratón. Beautiful cobbled streets!

The exit from Sepúlveda follows an old medieval path through the solitary Puerta de la Fuerza, one of the seven original gates that the medieval wall had, and continues along the remains of the Roman road that goes down to the historic Picazos bridge, on the Duratón river. .
In this part of the river the path is narrow and there is a descent that reaches the river where on the other side, you can see the city of Sepúlveda.

Thanks to this simple construction, since time immemorial, the inhabitants near the town of Sepúlveda have been able to cross the river and approach the other side of the riverbed that crosses close to the town.
